---
title: "Citations Analysis - Padua Data"
author: "Lasse Hjorth Madsen"
date: "2026-04-14"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true 
toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Overview

This notebook analyzes the relationship between citations and years in company using the Padua merged dataset.

```{r}
#| label: setup
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(GGally)
library(quanteda)
library(quanteda.textstats)
theme_set(theme_minimal())
```

```{r}
#| label: load_data

# Works both interactively (wd = project root) and when rendering (wd = qmd folder)
data_dir <- if (file.exists("Padua_All_Data_Merged_09April.xlsx")) "." else "ad-hoc/Padua"

# Read the Excel file
df <- read_excel(file.path(data_dir, "Padua_All_Data_Merged_09April.xlsx"))
```

# Data Cleaning

```{r}
#| label: data_cleaning

# Convert to numeric and handle outliers
df <- df |>
  mutate(
    # Convert to numeric
    across(c(`Years in Company`, Citations, Connections, `Years in Position`, `h-index`), as.numeric),
    # Replace outlier values with NA
    `Years in Company` = if_else(`Years in Company` >= 80, NA_real_, `Years in Company`),
    Citations = if_else(Citations >= 20000, NA_real_, Citations),
    Connections = if_else(Connections >= 5000, NA_real_, Connections)
  )

cat("Rows available:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Rows with valid Citations:", sum(!is.na(df$Citations)), "\n")
cat("Rows with valid Years in Company:", sum(!is.na(df$`Years in Company`)), "\n")

# Show summary statistics
df |>
  select(`Years in Company`, Citations, Connections, `Years in Position`, `h-index`) |>
  summary()
```

# Citations vs Years in Company

```{r}
#| label: main_plot
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6

df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = `Years in Company`, y = Citations)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "steelblue") +
  xlim(c(0, 45)) + ylim(c(0, 10000)) +
  labs(
    title = "Citations vs Years in Company",
    subtitle = "Linear trend with confidence interval",
    x = "Years in Company",
    y = "Citations"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )
```

# Multiple Predictors of Citations

```{r}
#| label: faceted_predictors
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 10

# Select potential predictor variables
predictor_vars <- c(
  "Connections",
  "Years in Position",
  "Years in Company",
  "h-index"
)

# Create long format for faceted plotting
df_long <- df |>
  select(Citations, all_of(predictor_vars)) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(predictor_vars),
    names_to = "Predictor",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(Value), !is.na(Citations))

# Create faceted plot
df_long |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Value, y = Citations)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~Predictor, scales = "free", ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Citations vs Multiple Predictors",
    subtitle = "Each panel shows relationship with a different predictor variable",
    x = "Value",
    y = "Citations"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10)
  )
```

# Correlation Matrix

```{r}
#| label: correlation_matrix

# Calculate correlations for all predictors
cors <- df |>
  select(Citations, all_of(predictor_vars)) |>
  cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Extract correlations with Citations and sort
cors[, "Citations"] |>
  sort(decreasing = TRUE) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  setNames("Correlation with Citations") |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 3)
```

# Scatterplot Matrix

```{r}
#| label: scatterplot_matrix
#| column: page
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 12

# Create scatterplot matrix
ggpairs(
  df |> select(Citations, all_of(predictor_vars)),
  title = "Scatterplot Matrix: Citations and Predictors",
  lower = list(continuous = wrap("points", alpha = 0.3, size = 0.5)),
  diag = list(continuous = wrap("densityDiag", alpha = 0.5)),
  upper = list(continuous = wrap("cor", size = 4))
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 9)
  )
```

# Bigram Frequencies (TF-IDF)

```{r}
#| label: bigram_helper

top_bigrams <- function(text, n = 20) {
  toks <- text |>
    na.omit() |>
    corpus() |>
    tokens(remove_punct = TRUE, remove_numbers = TRUE, remove_symbols = TRUE) |>
    tokens_tolower() |>
    tokens_remove(pattern = c(stopwords("en"), stopwords("it"), "|", "+", "-"))

  bi_dfm <- toks |>
    tokens_ngrams(n = 2, concatenator = " ") |>
    dfm()

  bi_tfidf <- bi_dfm |> dfm_tfidf()

  tibble(
    feature = featnames(bi_tfidf),
    tfidf   = colSums(bi_tfidf)
  ) |>
    arrange(desc(tfidf)) |>
    slice_head(n = n)
}

plot_bigrams <- function(text, col_name) {
  top_bigrams(text, n = 20) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = tfidf, y = reorder(feature, tfidf))) +
    geom_point() +
    labs(
      title = paste("Top 20 bigrams in", col_name, "(TF-IDF weighted)"),
      x = "TF-IDF (sum across documents)",
      y = NULL
    ) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13))
}
```

## Job Description

```{r}
#| label: bigrams_job_description
#| fig-height: 6
plot_bigrams(df[["Job Description"]], "Job Description")
```

## Profile Headline

```{r}
#| label: bigrams_profile_headline
#| fig-height: 6
plot_bigrams(df[["Profile Headline"]], "Profile Headline")
```

## LI Current Job

```{r}
#| label: bigrams_li_current_job
#| fig-height: 6
plot_bigrams(df[["LI Current Job"]], "LI Current Job")
```
